dialogueChunks.intro: It's done, {player_name}. {quest_threat} left at dawn with nothing but the clothes on their back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the evidence was presented, even the loyal guards turned against them. {target_npc} read the inventory records aloud: every diverted crate, every lie. The people voted unanimously: banishment, and a new council will govern. You helped us {quest_action}, and {quest_stakes} are free to govern themselves for the first time in years.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this: it was liberated from {quest_focus}. It belongs to the people, and the people want you to have it.

playerResponses.text: A settlement governing itself. That's how it should always have been.
I'm glad the truth came out. Use your freedom wisely.

dialogueChunks.intro: The trading post is open, {player_name}. Officially, permanently, and defiantly open.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The first independent merchants arrived this morning at half the consortium's prices. {target_npc} is managing operations with a grin so wide I'm worried their face will crack. Three other settlements have declared support. You helped us {quest_action}, and {quest_stakes} everywhere are finally free to trade fairly.
dialogueChunks.outro: Free trade. Fair prices. Open roads. You made this possible. Take this with the gratitude of every independent trader in the territory.

playerResponses.text: A free market spreading across the region? That's a revolution worth fighting for.
Good to see the consortium humbled. Keep the post running.

dialogueChunks.intro: The warden is in chains, {player_name}. Actual chains, in the very cell where they held {target_npc}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the workers testified, the entire settlement turned. Even the personal guard laid down their weapons. {quest_focus} is broken: {target_npc} led a delegation to the border and made it clear that no more labor would be provided. You helped us {quest_action}, and {quest_stakes} are home with their families for the first time in months.
dialogueChunks.outro: Justice isn't always clean, but this time it was thorough. Take this: you earned it by standing with people who had no one else.

playerResponses.text: The workers are home and the warden is in chains. That's the right ending.
Make sure the tribunal is fair. Even tyrants deserve due process.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. You should see this place right now. It doesn't even look the same.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} is abolished. The barracks have been converted into a community hall. {target_npc} was elected representative by unanimous vote. The soldiers who stayed accepted the new arrangement. There was a feast last night: the first where people ate without guilt or fear. You helped us {quest_action}, and {quest_stakes} finally know what freedom tastes like.
dialogueChunks.outro: We ate freely and laughed freely for the first time since anyone can remember. This is yours: a token of a people who remembered their dignity.

playerResponses.text: A feast without fear. That's what you were fighting for all along.
Freedom earned by the people themselves. I was just the spark.
